Tests that `Math.asin` returns `NaN` for input slightly less than -1.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.asin` function in JavaScript. It checks that the function returns `NaN` (Not-a-Number) when given an input slightly less than -1, specifically `-1.000000000000001`. The use of `assert` ensures that the test will fail if `Math.asin` does not return `NaN` for this input, which is outside the valid domain of the arcsine function. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific edge case for the `Math.asin` function.
